items.title: Total Net Points | NBA Finals
items.author: Hoop Informatics
items.body_excerpt:
Total Net Points | NBA Finals 

How many points a player was estimated to add to their teams scoring margin in the series 

1️⃣ +19.9  OG Anunoby
2️⃣ +14.2  Karl-Anthony Towns
3️⃣ +12.4  Victor Wembanyama
—————————————
😵 -17.6  De’Aaron Fox

Learn more: espnanalytics.com/net-pts-expl...

images.alt: Horizontal bar chart titled Knicks vs Spurs, Total Net Points, NBA Finals, Knicks Win Series 4-1, data from ESPN Analytics. Bars run from highest to lowest net points. OG Anunoby leads at +19.9, followed by Karl-Anthony Towns at +14.2, both Knicks. Victor Wembanyama is at +12.4 for the Spurs. Josh Hart is at +9.7 for the Knicks. Devin Vassell is at +7.7 for the Spurs. Jalen Brunson is at +7.6 for the Knicks. Dylan Harper is at +5.2 for the Spurs. Mikal Bridges is at +4.9 for the Knicks. Julian Champagnie is at +0.8 for the Spurs. Ariel Hukporti is at +0.7 for the Knicks. Jeremy Sochan is at -0.7 for the Spurs. Luke Kornet is at -2.9 for the Spurs. Jordan Clarkson is at -3.2 and Jose Alvarado at -3.5, both Knicks. Harrison Barnes is at -3.6, Carter Bryant at -4.0, and Keldon Johnson at -4.3, all Spurs. Stephon Castle is at -7.1 for the Spurs. Mitchell Robinson is at -11.0, Miles McBride at -11.5, and Landry Shamet at -13.6, all Knicks. De’Aaron Fox is at -17.6 for the Spurs.

items.title: Congrats to Knicks fans everywhere!
items.author: AdamTaylorNBA
items.body_excerpt:
Congrats to Knicks fans everywhere! 

The franchise made some ballsy decisions along the way, and we’re proven right to do so! 

I’m genuinely happy for you all. The NBA is better when the Knicks are a force! 

However…the Celtics will be coming for you next season 😉

media.description: Seventeen years to the day before I was born, the New York Knicks won their first NBA championship. Three years and two days later, they won another. It took 53 years — 19,392 days, of which I was ali...

items.title: I Lived To See This
items.author: Last Night In Basketball
items.body_excerpt: Seventeen years to the day before I was born, the New York Knicks won their first NBA championship. Three years and two days later, they won another. It took 53 years — 19,392 days, of which I was alive for 14,281 of them — for it to happen again. After waiting my entire life, I…

items.title: Jalen Brunson scored 29 points in the second half of Game 5 last night
items.author: Tommy Beer
items.body_excerpt:
Jalen Brunson scored 29 points in the second half of Game 5 last night 

That’s the MOST POINTS scored in the second half of any NBA Finals game played in the NBA’s modern era.

Brunson did it in a Finals-clinching win on the road to break a 53-year title drought for the Knicks

items.title: June 14, 1987: James Worthy with the hustle play, Magic Johnson with the slam during the Lakers' 106-93 win over Boston in Game 6 of the NBA Finals.
items.author: NBA Cobwebs
items.body_excerpt:
June 14, 1987: James Worthy with the hustle play, Magic Johnson with the slam during the Lakers' 106-93 win over Boston in Game 6 of the NBA Finals.

Worthy scored 22 PTS, Magic had 16 PTS/19 AST, and Kareem Abdul-Jabbar scored 32 PTS as the Lakers clinched the NBA Championship.

items.title: “At some point, the accumulation of winning trades and signings, firings and hirings, comebacks and blowouts, depth and adaptability, risks and restraints, is just the reality. And the reality is, these Knicks were Goliaths hiding in plain sight.”

items.author: Zach Kram

At long last, the Knicks' title drought is over. 

I wrote about Jalen Brunson's MVP-worthy finale, the unique way the Knicks built a championship roster, and what a disappointing end means for the rising Spurs.

items.title: This is pinned, but I guess I need to remind:
items.author: Jim Owczarski
items.body_excerpt:
This is pinned, but I guess I need to remind:

I do not do any reporting on social media. 

I have opinions, I’ll reply with thoughts, I troll, I use sarcasm and generally have a good time.

If I didn’t write it for jsonline.com …then I really don’t know what to tell ya…

items.title: The Knicks broke all the rules and won everything
items.author: Good Morning It's Basketball
items.body_excerpt: With a small guard as their best player, a flighty center anchoring a still impressive defense and a thin bench, the Knicks beat everyone to bring a championship back to New York. Good morning. It's the final newsletter of the 2025-26 NBA season. Let's basketball. The belief…

They are joined by Indiana Pacers legend Roy Hibbert. They react to the New York Knicks becoming NBA…

items.title: My biggest takeaway from the Knicks winning is we all have to stop with sweeping declarations about team-building. “You can’t win when a small guard is your best player.” Actually, you can if he’s awesome and you build a proper team around him.

items.body_excerpt: My biggest takeaway from the Knicks winning is we all have to stop with sweeping declarations about team-building. “You can’t win when a small guard is your best player.” Actually, you can if he’s awesome and you build a proper team around him.

media.title: Knicks' first championship in 53 years a product of trust and sacrifice
media.description: Shortly before a historic playoff run, Knicks owner James Dolan spoke to his team and gave a message of sacrifice. Ten weeks later, and the New York Knicks are NBA champions.

items.title: Jalen Brunson last night:
items.author: Justin Kubatko
items.body_excerpt:
Jalen Brunson last night:

✅ 45 PTS
✅ 14-27 FG
✅ 4-7 3P
✅ 13-15 FT

Brunson is the fourth player to score at least 45 points in a Finals-clinching win.

He scored 47.9% of New York's points, the fifth-highest such figure in a Finals game.

Read, share, subscribe ⬇️

items.title: One lesson from the NBA Finals:
items.author: Iztok Franko
items.body_excerpt:
One lesson from the NBA Finals:

A heliocentric model can work.

Jalen Brunson just proved it.

For the Lakers the challenge isn't the star. It's finding the multidimensional supporting cast around him👇
